text:
On a moonlit night, under a canvas of stars, a small rabbit hopped nervously along a forest path. It had heard tales of the Whisper Woods, places where time seemed to pause, and songs that danced like fireflies.

As it went, it encountered an old Oak tree. The rabbit stopped and looked up. The tree wasn't just old—it had lived through countless seasons. Its branches swayed gently in the breeze.

The tree spoke. "The songs you hear? They're echoes from memories of seasons past. There's no magic in them—just the rustling leaves and soft winds. Just remember, it's okay not to hop along alone."

The rabbit, named Marlene after a forgotten great-grandmother, listened intently. It found comfort in the conversation and decided to continue further into the woods, its heart slightly less fearful.
